macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: Fed Governor Waller Cautious on Rate Cuts Amid Iran Conflict and Surging Inflation
items.summary: Fed Governor Waller highlighted strong business investment offsetting weak consumer spending, with Q1 GDP growth at 2.4% annualized, but warned of elevated inflation risks from the Iran conflict and Strait of Hormuz closure, projecting March PCE headline inflation at 3.5% and core at 3.2%. He favors delaying rate cuts until later in the year to support the labor market once the outlook stabilizes.[1]

items.title: Strait of Hormuz Closure Drives Global Energy Price Surge and Inflation Fears
items.summary: The ongoing conflict with Iran has closed the Strait of Hormuz, causing a 10.8% jump in energy CPI, boosting 12-month headline inflation to 3.3% and core to 2.6%, with broader impacts on gasoline, natural gas, fertilizer, and helium prices leading to rationing in Europe and Asia.[1][2]

items.title: Economic Outlook Improves as Prolonged Strait of Hormuz Disruption Averted
items.summary: Markets brighten with reduced risk of extended crude oil, LNG, fertilizer, and commodity supply disruptions from the Iran conflict, potentially easing inflationary pressures after initial global price spikes.[3]

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Bitcoin Surges Past $78,000, Boosting MicroStrategy Stock
items.summary: Bitcoin reached $78,209, up 4.7% in 24 hours, driving MicroStrategy (Strategy) shares up 13.83% to $169.54 amid market recovery linked to positive U.S.-Iran negotiations. The company holds 780,897 BTC valued at $60.8 billion.

items.title: Crypto Futures Liquidations Hit $745 Million in 24 Hours
items.summary: Crypto futures saw $745 million in liquidations across major exchanges over the past 24 hours, with $150 million in the last hour alone, reflecting high market volatility.

items.title: Bitcoin Holds Near $75,000 Amid ETF Inflows and Geopolitical Tensions
items.summary: Bitcoin traded around $74,856-$75,000 supported by ETF inflows despite volatility from geopolitical uncertainty, while Ethereum stayed above $2,300.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: Anthropic Releases Claude Opus 4.7 with Enhanced Vision and Coding
items.summary: Anthropic launched Claude Opus 4.7, featuring improved visual reasoning, software coding, and overall performance, positioning it between Claude 4.6 and the unreleased Mythos preview. The model uses a new high reasoning level for better reliability on complex tasks.

items.title: OpenAI Updates Codex with Advanced Computer Use and Browser Integration
items.summary: OpenAI released an update to Codex, adding better computer use capabilities, an integrated browser, and new tools to expand its functionality across various applications.

items.title: Chinese Firms Tencent and Alibaba Launch Open-Source 3D World Generation AI
items.summary: Tencent and Alibaba introduced free open-source AI tools that generate entire 3D worlds from text prompts, surpassing Google's Genie and enabling direct exports to game engines for developers and filmmakers.

items.title: AI-Generated Val Kilmer Stars in New Film After His 2025 Death
items.summary: Filmmakers debuted clips from 'As Deep as the Grave' featuring an AI-resurrected version of Val Kilmer, who died in 2025, with approval from his children.

items.title: Kroger and Others Use AI to Sell Perishable Food and Cut Waste
items.summary: Grocery chains like Kroger are deploying AI tools from Flashfood across 2000 North American stores to price and sell near-expired perishables, boosting sales and providing consumer behavior insights amid inflation.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: Meta Raises Prices on Quest VR Headsets Due to AI-Driven Chip Shortages
items.summary: Meta is increasing U.S. prices for Quest 3S and Quest 3 VR headsets starting April 19, citing surging memory chip costs from AI data-center demand by OpenAI, Google, and Microsoft. Similar price hikes have affected Dell, HP, Microsoft, and Sony's PlayStation 5.

items.title: Quantum Motion Deploys First Full-Stack Silicon Quantum Computer
items.summary: Quantum Motion has achieved a milestone by deploying the first full-stack quantum computer using standard silicon chip fabrication processes, potentially accelerating scalable quantum systems.

items.title: Sequoia Leaders Raise $7 Billion for Major Tech Investments
items.summary: New leaders at Sequoia have secured about $7 billion, the firm's largest fund yet, to make significant bets in the tech sector amid ongoing industry growth.

items.title: OnlyFans Nears Minority Stake Sale Valuing Platform at $3 Billion
items.summary: Following the death of owner Leonid Radvinsky, OnlyFans is in advanced talks with Architect Capital to sell a minority stake, potentially valuing the adult content platform at over $3 billion.

items.title: US Senator Warren Raises Concerns Over Nvidia's Acquisition of SchedMD
items.summary: Senator Elizabeth Warren has expressed national security and competition worries about Nvidia's purchase of SchedMD, which controls Slurm software critical for US government supercomputers.

items.title: Google and Qualcomm Push Android PCs into Mainstream
items.summary: Google and Qualcomm are advancing Android PCs to challenge Microsoft and Apple, aiming to extend the dominant mobile OS to desktops and reshape personal computing.
